skill.name: peer-review
skill.description: Guides structured peer review of scientific manuscripts, grants, and presentations using methodology, statistics, ethics, reproducibility, and reporting-standard checklists.
skill.summary: Create rigorous, constructive scientific peer reviews with checklist-based assessment.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk weak-cryptography and reconnaissance patterns, but review showed these are false positives from scientific review terminology and reference links. The remaining concern is legitimate but elevated: this community skill allows Bash and includes local Python command examples for schematic generation and slide conversion, so users should review commands before execution.

medium_findings.title: Bash-Enabled Workflow Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash as an allowed tool and includes command examples that run local Python scripts for schematic generation and PDF-to-image conversion. This is not malicious, but it increases risk because a community skill may cause command execution against user-provided files.
medium_findings.recommendation: Publish with a warning that users should inspect commands and referenced scripts before allowing Bash execution, especially for untrusted PDFs or file paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and Bash permission are directly visible. Confidence is not higher because the examples target local workflow helpers and do not show exfiltration or destructive behavior.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs Flagged as Network Activity
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are citations to reporting standards such as CONSORT, STROBE, PRISMA, and other scientific guidelines. They are documentation references, not network requests or hidden callbacks.
low_findings.recommendation: Keep the references, but make clear in marketplace metadata that these are external documentation links.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain plain reference links only. No code path, fetch call, credential access, or automated network behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography alerts correspond to peer-review terms such as methods, design, confidence intervals, controls, and reporting standards. No cryptographic API, hash routine, cipher selection, or password handling was found.
low_findings.recommendation: Dismiss these static hits as false positives caused by domain language in scientific review checklists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found checklist prose, not crypto implementation. The evidence supports a false-positive assessment across the sampled flagged files.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Scientific Review Language
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance alerts are caused by benign phrases about statistical tests, search strategy, scanner parameters, and review workflows. No evidence found of host enumeration, port scanning, or network discovery instructions.
low_findings.recommendation: Dismiss these alerts as false positives after semantic review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are peer-review guidance and reporting checklist language. No reconnaissance tooling or operational discovery workflow was present.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Invocation Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows Bash examples that invoke Python scripts. These commands are operationally useful but should be treated as a risky pattern in a community skill because users may run them on local files.
dangerous_patterns.recommendation: Require user confirmation before command execution and avoid running helper scripts that have not been reviewed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command snippets are explicit and easy to verify. They appear benign, so the risk is execution exposure rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Scientific reviews often miss methodology, statistics, and reporting gaps. This skill provides a structured checklist workflow for constructive manuscript, grant, and presentation review.
actual_capabilities: Guides section-by-section review of scientific manuscripts and grants.
Checks methodology, statistics, ethics, reproducibility, and data availability.
Maps study types to reporting standards such as CONSORT, STROBE, PRISMA, and ARRIVE.
Helps draft structured summaries, major concerns, minor issues, and recommendations.
Provides presentation review guidance with slide-level visual and content checks.
limitations: Does not replace expert domain judgment or journal editorial policy.
Cannot verify raw data integrity without access to source files and context.
May require external scripts for optional schematic or slide image workflows.
Reference guidance is broad and may not cover every discipline-specific standard.

use_cases.title: Journal Manuscript Review
use_cases.description: Evaluate a submitted manuscript for rigor, clarity, reproducibility, ethics, and reporting completeness.

use_cases.title: Grant Proposal Assessment
use_cases.description: Assess research design, feasibility, statistical plans, expected impact, and constructive revision priorities.

use_cases.title: Preprint Improvement Review
use_cases.description: Identify weaknesses before journal submission and turn them into specific revision actions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Manuscript Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a manuscript draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this manuscript draft. Summarize the main claim, list major concerns, list minor concerns, and suggest a publication recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Methods and Statistics Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate the methods and statistical analysis in this manuscript. Check sample size, controls, assumptions, missing data, and reproducibility details.

prompt_templates.title: Reporting Standards Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Identify the relevant reporting guideline for this study type. Check whether the manuscript satisfies the key required reporting elements.

prompt_templates.title: Full Reviewer Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a formal peer-review report with summary, strengths, major issues, minor issues, required revisions, and a clear editorial recommendation.

output_examples.input: A randomized trial manuscript with unclear allocation concealment and no trial registration statement.
output_examples.output: The review highlights trial design concerns, requests allocation details, asks for registration information, and recommends major revision before acceptance.

output_examples.input: A grant proposal with a strong premise but weak power calculation and limited contingency plans.
output_examples.output: The response separates strengths from feasibility risks and suggests clearer sample-size justification, backup analyses, and milestone criteria.

output_examples.input: A conference slide deck with dense text and small figure labels.
output_examples.output: The review gives slide-level readability feedback, flags accessibility issues, and recommends focused visual revisions before presentation.

best_practices: Provide the manuscript type, target journal or panel criteria, and review scope before starting.
Separate major validity issues from minor clarity or formatting issues.
Ask for evidence-based feedback with specific manuscript sections or slide numbers.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to replace specialist statistical or ethical review when stakes are high.
Do not request extra experiments unless they are necessary for the central claim.
Do not run optional Bash workflows without reviewing the referenced scripts and file paths.

faq.question: Can this skill write a complete reviewer report?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure a formal report with summary, strengths, major issues, minor issues, and recommendation.

faq.question: Does it support grant reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for evaluating research applications, feasibility, methodology, and constructive feedback.

faq.question: Which reporting standards does it reference?
faq.answer: It references common standards including CONSORT, STROBE, PRISMA, ARRIVE, MIAME, MINSEQE, and related guidelines.

faq.question: Can it verify statistical correctness?
faq.answer: It can identify common statistical issues, but expert statistical review is still needed for complex analyses.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with confidential manuscripts?
faq.answer: Use normal confidentiality controls for your AI environment. The skill itself contains guidance and local references, not an upload service.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill allows Bash and includes optional local command examples. Review commands before running them on local files.
